subject: [RFC PATCH 20/45] mm: page_alloc: aggressively pack non-movable allocations in tainted SPBs on large systems

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new threshold, SPB_AGGRESSIVE_THRESHOLD, to control when the kernel aggressively packs non-movable allocations into tainted superpageblocks (SPBs) on large systems. On systems with more than 8 superpageblocks, it relaxes the minimum order floor for tainted SPBs, allowing non-movable allocations to consume free space at any granularity. This is done to prevent unnecessary tainting of clean SPBs and preserve MOVABLE capacity within tainted SPBs. The patch preserves the original behavior on small systems with fewer than 8 superpageblocks.
